info.name: Kachō VPC QA Suite
info.description: VPC-domain regression suite (Network, Subnet, Address, RouteTable, SecurityGroup, Gateway, PrivateEndpoint, NetBox sync). Split from kacho-test/collections/kacho-qa.postman_collection.json.

item.name: NET-CR-OK — Network create returns Operation envelope
item.description:
Compare YC Operation envelope shape with Kachō.
Expected YC fields: id (base32, NOT prefixed like vpc_), description='Create network' (human),
createdBy (subject IAM id), modifiedAt, metadata.@type starts with 'type.googleapis.com/yandex.cloud...'.
Kachō uses UUID + prefix vpc_, description='Create network <UUID>', createdBy='anonymous',
metadata.@type starts with 'type.googleapis.com/kacho.cloud...'.
Status: documented format-diff (YC-DIFF-OPERATION-SHAPE).

item.name: NET-CR-NAME-ACCEPTS — Network name validation rules
item.description:
YC vpc:Network does NOT validate name pattern strictly: 'BadName' accepted as-is.
Kachō rejects uppercase letters with HTTP 400 fieldViolations[name].
Status: Kachō stricter — documented in YC-DIFF-NAME-VALIDATION.md.
This case asserts YC's permissive behavior.

item.name: NET-LIST — Network list response shape
item.description:
Compare YC vpc:Network list response shape with Kachō.
Expected from YC: networks[].id is base32 (~20 chars), createdAt RFC3339, fields camelCase, defaultSecurityGroupId present (Kachō missing).
Status: matches structurally; some YC-only fields documented as deviation.

item.name: NET-LIST-PAGE-TOKEN-FORMAT — Network list nextPageToken format
item.description:
Expected YC: nextPageToken is opaque base64-ish string (long ~190 chars).
Kachō: opaque base64 too — but content-format differs (Kachō encodes timestamp:uuid).
Status: opaque tokens are intentionally opaque — no contract requirement on format.

item.name: NET-LIST-PAGE-TOKEN-LEAK — pageToken does not leak server internals
item.description:
Expected YC: nextPageToken is opaque (no plaintext IDs / timestamps).
Kachō: nextPageToken is base64 of '<timestamp>:<uuid>' — leaks ordering keys.
Status: documented in finding PAGE-TOKEN-LEAK.md (Kachō).

item.name: VPC-LIST-FILTER — filter query-param parsed and applied
item.description:
Findings: FILTER-EXPR-IGNORED.md | Status: Kachō decision (known-gap)
Techniques: Negative path, Equivalence Partitioning

Probe (2026-05-04): YC returns 400 INVALID_ARGUMENT on garbage `filter` expr ('Bad expression at column 1...'); Kachō silently accepts it as 200 with full unfiltered list. Documented as known-gap in FILTER-EXPR-IGNORED.md — proto field exists but parser is not implemented. Test locks current Kachō behaviour so future implementation triggers breaking-change signal.

item.description:
Findings: LIST-LABEL-SELECTOR-MISSING.md | Status: Kachō decision (silent-accept)
Techniques: Negative path, Boundary

Probe (2026-05-04): YC VPC API does NOT have labelSelector at all (returns 404 if you build a different URL). Kachō accepts the query-param silently and returns full list. Documented as known-gap in LIST-LABEL-SELECTOR-MISSING.md — labelSelector is not part of YC VPC contract, so silent-accept is acceptable.

item.name: VPC-LOCALIZED-ERRORS-MISSING — Accept-Language ignored, no LocalizedMessage detail
item.description:
Findings: LOCALIZED-ERRORS-MISSING.md | Status: Kachō decision
Techniques: Negative path, Equivalence Partitioning

Probe (2026-05-04): YC returns details[] with LocalizedMessage (locale=en) + RequestInfo + (no BadRequest); Kachō returns BadRequest only. Accept-Language is parsed by YC (always returns en) and ignored by Kachō. Kachō structured BadRequest is machine-readable verbatim, while YC's LocalizedMessage is text-formatted; both approaches valid.
